programme.title: Wild Shots
programme.desc: Host Tim Harmsen teams up with SEAL Team 6 legend Don Mann to tackle real-world defense tactics and home-clearing techniques. A visit to Civil War battlefields connects history to modern freedoms, while the brutal 5-Year AR Challenge pushes an AR-15 through 10,000 rounds without cleaning. Will the rifle survive—or fail under fire?

programme.title: Missing
programme.desc: Missing

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The competition reaches its ultimate test as the Face-Off enters Phase Two. Contestants are pushed to their breaking points with a brutal mix of tactical and physical challenges—running and gunning through the forest, navigating five tactical evolutions, and grinding through five grueling physical trials, capped by a punishing two-mile run over unforgiving terrain. Along the way, Don reveals the origins of his legendary SEAL Adventure Challenge, the inspiration behind this ultimate proving ground. When one contestant’s body shuts down in shock, the resilience of the warrior mindset is put on full display in a dramatic fight to recover and press on. In this season finale, only the strongest will endure, but every competitor leaves forged by fire.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Ken Shamrock’s Iron Man competition comes to a head as two contestants battle it out for the number one spot. Every strike, every takedown matters—because only one can walk away crowned the victor. But there’s no time to celebrate. It’s mission day. After a quick live-fire break, the team receives critical intel from the ISR daylight crew. The risk spikes as night falls and the mission is suddenly altered, forcing the contestants to adapt on the fly. With Red Cat providing coordination from above, the squad is redeployed under new marching orders and even higher stakes. A partially compromised team makes survival uncertain, but when the moment of truth arrives, the team leader takes decisive action—leading a direct breach on the target. One bold decision shifts the course of the mission entirely, and its success now hangs by a thread.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: With the mission complete, the contestants think the season is winding down—but HQ has other plans. Their once-fabricated cover story becomes reality, pulling them into a pulse-pounding new challenge designed to test strength, coordination, and mastery of weaponry. This hybrid competition pushes every skill to the limit, weeding out all but the most well-rounded tactical athletes. Along the way, the legendary Don Mann shares his own backstory, giving rare insight into the life and mindset of the man behind the mission. When the dust settles, only one competitor will rise above the rest—earning the title of the season’s most complete warrior.

programme.desc: This sequel to the popular spaghetti western "Clint the Stranger" was released four years after the success of the first film and essentially uses the same plot. George Martin returns as Clint (renamed Trinity in some countries), an ex-gunslinger desperately wanting to be forgiven and accepted by his family that he abandoned years earlier. One major differences in the plot when compared to the first film is the addition of Klaus Kinski as a ruthless bounty hunter. The bounty hunter tracks Clint down and forces him to return to his violent ways to protect himself and his family.
